---
title: "Figure 3—figure supplement 1 — per-step information vs score variance, and their calibration, across the 5 macro algorithms and 4 parameters"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
TIME-RESOLVED, PER-PARAMETER second-Bartlett check. For each of the four identified parameters
(k_on, k_off, i, N_ch) two stacked rows, four approximation columns (R, MR, VR, IR): the recursive
ladder, so recursion is held fixed and only the interval conditioning varies.

  INFO row (log10 y): the per-step Gaussian Fisher information diagonal E[I_t] (blue) and the
    score variance Var(s_t) (orange). They coincide wherever the filter is calibrated; both
    vanish off-agonist and (k_on / N_ch, recursive filters) in the decay, while i keeps a
    conductance-noise floor.
  LOG J/F row (linear, null 0): the per-step calibration log10(Var(s_t)/E[I_t]) with a 90%
    bootstrap CI over the 1000 recordings. A calibrated filter sits on 0 at every step. The
    per-step diagonal is roughly calibrated for all algorithms; the J != F that separates them
    lives in the CROSS-time score correlation (Figure 3 rows E and F), not here. THIS FIGURE IS THE
    PREMISE, Figure 3 is the consequence: per step everyone reports its information correctly, so
    the accumulated failure cannot be a per-sample modelling error.

Reads the same 5 ensemble CSVs figures/data/figure_3_time_dlik_{NR,MR,IR,R,NMR}.csv (each ~1 GB,
1000 sims; cell N_ch=100, Current_Noise=1e-4, interval=0.1 tau) ONCE each. Per step:
  I_t,kk = (d y_mean/d theta_k)^2 / y_var + 0.5 (d y_var/d theta_k)^2 / y_var^2   (Gaussian FIM
diagonal, validated: sum_t I_t reproduces the dumped global FIM to machine precision); the score
s_t,k is the stored d logL_t/d theta_k. Time axis in milliseconds. The data-file key "NMR" is the
algorithm shown to readers as "MNR". A point is shown wherever the per-step FIM is non-zero
(F > 1e-6); genuine zeros (sensitivity = 0, ratio 0/0) are dropped. The writer emits every
evolution row TWICE (a segment-blank copy + a segment_index=0 copy, identical values); collapsed
by mean per (sim, sample).

RENDER: reads ~5 GB, too heavy for a laptop; run where figures/data/ resolves.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(patchwork)
# A-strict roster: the recursive ladder (papers/1_method/decisions.md). NR and NMR remain in the
# dumps and can be restored by putting their keys back here; nothing else needs to change.
ALGOS <- c("R", "MR", "VR", "IR")                    # data-file keys
ALAB  <- c(NR = "NR", NMR = "MNR", R = "R", MR = "MR", VR = "VR", IR = "IR")  # display (NMR -> MNR)
FULL  <- c(NR = "Non-Recursive", NMR = "Mean Non-Recursive", R = "Recursive",
           MR = "Mean Recursive", VR = "Variance Recursive", IR = "Interval Recursive")
LEAD  <- ALGOS[1]     # leftmost column: carries the y title, the row titles and the panel tags
PIDX  <- c(0, 1, 2, 5)                               # on, off, unitary_current, Num_ch_mean
PLAB  <- c("k[on]", "k[off]", "i", "N[ch]")          # plotmath row labels
PTTL  <- list(bquote(k[on] * "  (opening rate)"), bquote(k[off] * "  (closing rate)"),
              bquote(i * "  (unitary current)"),    bquote(N[ch] * "  (channel number)"))
FLOOR  <- 1e-6                                        # show wherever the per-step FIM is non-zero
B      <- 200                                        # bootstrap replicates over recordings
YLIM   <- c(-0.5, 0.5)                               # log-ratio (log J/F) linear zoom, null 0
YCURVE <- c(1e-3, 1e3)                               # common log y-range for the two info curves
```

```{r read}
col1  <- function(d) d[, .(v = mean(value)), by = .(simulation_index, sample_index)]
col1p <- function(d) d[, .(v = mean(value)), by = .(simulation_index, sample_index, param_index)]

read_si <- function(a) {
  dt <- fread(sprintf("../data/figure_3_time_dlik_%s.csv", a), skip = 1, showProgress = FALSE)
  yv  <- col1 (dt[component_path == "y_var"  & calculus == "primitive"  & !is.na(sample_index)])
  dym <- col1p(dt[component_path == "y_mean" & calculus == "derivative" & param_index %in% PIDX & !is.na(sample_index)])
  dyv <- col1p(dt[component_path == "y_var"  & calculus == "derivative" & param_index %in% PIDX & !is.na(sample_index)])
  sco <- col1p(dt[component_path == "logL"   & calculus == "derivative" & param_index %in% PIDX & !is.na(sample_index)])
  setnames(yv, "v", "yv"); setnames(dym, "v", "dym"); setnames(dyv, "v", "dyv"); setnames(sco, "v", "s")
  tm <- unique(dt[!is.na(step_middle) & !is.na(sample_index), .(sample_index, t = step_middle, agonist)])
  rm(dt); gc(verbose = FALSE)
  X <- merge(merge(dym, dyv, by = c("simulation_index", "sample_index", "param_index")),
             sco, by = c("simulation_index", "sample_index", "param_index"))
  X <- merge(X, yv, by = c("simulation_index", "sample_index"))
  X[, I := dym^2 / yv + 0.5 * dyv^2 / yv^2]           # per-step Gaussian FIM diagonal
  list(si = X[, .(algo = a, param_index, simulation_index, sample_index, s, I)], tm = tm)
}

raw  <- lapply(ALGOS, read_si)
SI   <- rbindlist(lapply(raw, `[[`, "si"))
tmap <- raw[[1]]$tm
tmap[, t := t * 1000]                                # step_middle seconds -> ms
```

```{r compute-bootstrap}
set.seed(1)
cMean <- function(M) colMeans(M, na.rm = TRUE)
cVar  <- function(M) { n <- colSums(!is.na(M)); m <- colMeans(M, na.rm = TRUE)
                       (colMeans(M * M, na.rm = TRUE) - m^2) * n / (n - 1) }

facet_ratio <- function(d) {
  Sm  <- as.matrix(dcast(d, simulation_index ~ sample_index, value.var = "s")[, -1])
  Im  <- as.matrix(dcast(d, simulation_index ~ sample_index, value.var = "I")[, -1])
  smp <- as.integer(colnames(Sm)); n <- nrow(Sm)
  Ft  <- cMean(Im); ratio <- cVar(Sm) / Ft
  reps <- matrix(NA_real_, B, ncol(Sm))
  for (b in seq_len(B)) {
    idx <- sample.int(n, n, replace = TRUE)
    reps[b, ] <- cVar(Sm[idx, , drop = FALSE]) / cMean(Im[idx, , drop = FALSE])
  }
  lrep <- log10(reps); lrep[!is.finite(lrep)] <- NA
  data.table(sample_index = smp, F = Ft, lr = log10(ratio),
             lo = apply(lrep, 2, quantile, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
             hi = apply(lrep, 2, quantile, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
}

R <- SI[, facet_ratio(.SD), by = .(algo, param_index)]
R <- merge(R, tmap, by = "sample_index")
R <- R[is.finite(lr) & F > FLOOR]                    # drop only genuine zeros (0/0)
R[, `:=`(algo  = factor(algo, levels = ALGOS),
         param = factor(PLAB[match(param_index, PIDX)], levels = PLAB))]
```

```{r curves-data}
# The two raw info curves (no ratio, no 0/0): per-step FIM diagonal E[I_t] and score variance
# Var(s_t), which coincide where the filter is calibrated.
agg <- SI[, .(FIM = mean(I), score_var = var(s)), by = .(algo, param_index, sample_index)]
agg <- merge(agg, tmap[, .(sample_index, t)], by = "sample_index")
curves <- melt(agg, measure.vars = c("FIM", "score_var"),
               variable.name = "quantity", value.name = "val")
curves <- curves[is.finite(val) & val > FLOOR]
curves[, `:=`(algo  = factor(algo, levels = ALGOS),
              param = factor(PLAB[match(param_index, PIDX)], levels = PLAB),
              quantity = factor(quantity, levels = c("FIM", "score_var")))]
```

```{r layout, fig.width = 7.0, fig.height = 11}
FIMc <- "#0072B2"; SCVc <- "#D55E00"; NUL <- "#009E73"; RATc <- "#D55E00"
tr   <- range(tmap$t)
agon <- tmap[agonist > 0]; x0 <- min(agon$t); x1 <- max(agon$t)
# full-panel agonist band. On a log10 axis a negative floor is rejected (log10(<=0)=NaN drops the
# rect), so the info rows need a tiny positive floor; the linear log-ratio rows go negative.
mkshade <- function(logr = FALSE) annotate("rect", xmin = x0, xmax = x1,
                  ymin = if (logr) 1e-10 else -1e10, ymax = 1e10, fill = "grey85", alpha = .5)

colhead <- function(k) ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(ALAB[[k]], subtitle = FULL[[k]]) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = .5, size = 9), plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = .5, size = 7))

base <- function() theme_bw(base_size = 8)

# mode = "info" -> log10 magnitude axis (fixed decade breaks, no null); "linear" -> signed axis,
# 0 forced onto the ticks (the log J/F rows). y title only on NR; x numbers on every bottom-of-
# block panel, x TITLE only ONCE, on the centre column (R).
deco <- function(p, col, xr, ylim, yq, ttl = NULL, tag = NULL, bottom = FALSE, xlab = NULL, mode = "linear") {
  if (mode == "info") {
    p <- p + scale_y_log10(breaks = c(1e-2, 1, 100)) + coord_cartesian(xlim = xr, ylim = ylim)
  } else {
    brk <- pretty(ylim); brk <- sort(unique(c(0, brk))); brk <- brk[brk >= ylim[1] & brk <= ylim[2]]
    p <- p + scale_y_continuous(breaks = brk) + coord_cartesian(xlim = xr, ylim = ylim)
  }
  if (col == LEAD) p <- p + ylab(yq) + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8))
  else p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
  if (bottom) {
    if (col == "R") p <- p + xlab(xlab) + theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8))
    else p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
  } else p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())
  if (!is.null(tag)) p <- p + labs(tag = tag)
  if (!is.null(ttl)) p <- p + ggtitle(ttl) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8.5, face = "plain", margin = margin(b = 1)))
  # tight coupling of each info/ratio pair: info gets a small BOTTOM margin, ratio a small TOP
  # margin, so the pair sits together; the (larger) inter-parameter gap is kept on the far sides.
  mrg <- if (mode == "info") margin(1.5, 3, 0.3, 3) else margin(0.3, 3, 1.5, 3)
  p + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), plot.margin = mrg)
}

# INFO row panel: the two curves (FIM diagonal blue / score variance orange), log10
mk_info <- function(i, a, tag = NULL, bottom = FALSE) {
  d <- curves[param == PLAB[i] & algo == a]
  g <- ggplot(d, aes(t, val, colour = quantity)) + mkshade(TRUE) +
    geom_line(linewidth = .4) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(FIM = FIMc, score_var = SCVc), name = NULL,
                        labels = expression(F[t] == E * "[" * I[t] * "]" ~ "(Fisher information)",
                                            J[t] == Var(s[t]) ~ "(score variance)")) +
    base()
  deco(g, a, tr, YCURVE, "information", ttl = if (a == LEAD) PTTL[[i]] else NULL,
       tag = tag, bottom = bottom, xlab = "time (ms)", mode = "info")
}
# LOG J/F row panel: per-step calibration log10(Var(s_t)/E[I_t]) + 90% bootstrap CI, linear, null 0
mk_ratio <- function(i, a, tag = NULL, bottom = FALSE) {
  d <- R[param == PLAB[i] & algo == a]
  g <- ggplot(d, aes(t, lr)) + mkshade(FALSE) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = NUL, linewidth = .4) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = RATc, alpha = .25) +
    geom_line(colour = RATc, linewidth = .4) + base()
  deco(g, a, tr, YLIM, expression(log[10] ~ J[t] / F[t]), tag = tag, bottom = bottom, xlab = "time (ms)", mode = "linear")
}

info_tags  <- c("A", "C", "E", "G")
ratio_tags <- c("B", "D", "F", "H")
panels <- list()
for (i in seq_along(PLAB)) {
  for (a in ALGOS) panels <- c(panels, list(mk_info (i, a, tag = if (a == LEAD) info_tags[i]  else NULL)))
  for (a in ALGOS) panels <- c(panels, list(mk_ratio(i, a, tag = if (a == LEAD) ratio_tags[i] else NULL,
                                                      bottom = (i == length(PLAB)))))
}

# --- numbers the caption quotes, printed so they are never read off the plot (LINT-SRC) ---
# Per (algorithm, parameter): the median per-step log10(J/F) over the steps that carry information,
# and the fraction of those steps whose 90% bootstrap interval covers 0 (i.e. is calibrated).
.cap4 <- R[, .(median_log10_JF = median(lr),
               frac_CI_covers_0 = mean(lo <= 0 & hi >= 0),
               n_steps = .N), by = .(algo, param)]
setorder(.cap4, param, algo)
cat("\n=== Figure 4 caption numbers ===\n")
print(as.data.frame(.cap4), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
cat("overall fraction of (step, algo, param) whose CI covers 0:",
    sprintf("%.3f\n", R[, mean(lo <= 0 & hi >= 0)]))

g4 <- wrap_plots(c(lapply(ALGOS, colhead), panels), ncol = length(ALGOS),
                 heights = c(0.10, rep(c(1, 0.5), length(PLAB))), guides = "collect") &  # ratio rows half-height
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
ggsave("Figure_3_supplement_1.pdf", g4, width = 7.0, height = 11)
```
